messages.content: The five-number summary of a data set consists of the minimum, Q1 ,the mean, Q3, the maximum.

The five-number summary of a data set is typically represented as follows:

Minimum: The smallest value in the data set.
Q1 (First Quartile): The value that is greater than or equal to 25% of the data set.
Mean (or Median): The average value of the data set.
Q3 (Third Quartile): The value that is greater than or equal to 75% of the data set.
Maximum: The largest value in the data set. 

This summary provides a quick overview of the spread and distribution of the data set, allowing for easy comparison between different sets of data.
true or false The five-number summary of a data set consists of the minimum, Q1 ,the mean, Q3, the maximum.

False.

The five-number summary of a data set consists of the minimum, Q1 (First Quartile), the median (or mean), Q3 (Third Quartile), and the maximum. The mean is not typically included in the five-number summary, as it represents the average value of the data set rather than a specific point within the distribution.

Values which are extremely high or extremely low are called outliers.
Group of answer choices

True

False

True

Values which are extremely high or extremely low in a data set are called outliers. Outliers can significantly affect the interpretation and analysis of data, and may need to be handled or addressed separately in statistical analysis.

Measures of central tendency measure the center of the set of data in the distribution.
Group of answer choices

True

False

True

Measures of central tendency, such as the mean, median, and mode, are used to measure the center or average of a data set in a distribution. These measures provide a single value that represents the typical value or central point around which the data points are distributed.

The  mean, median and mode are the most commonly used measures of central tendency.
Group of answer choices

True

False

True

The mean, median, and mode are indeed the most commonly used measures of central tendency in statistics. These measures provide information about the average or central value of a set of data, helping to understand the distribution and characteristics of the data set.

The mean, median and mode for the set of data {3, 3, 5, 6, 8} are all the same value.
Group of answer choices

True

False

True

In the data set {3, 3, 5, 6, 8}, the mean, median, and mode are all equal to 5. This is because the mode is the most frequently occurring value and in this case, the mode is 3. Furthermore, the median is also 5 as it is the middle value when the data set is arranged in ascending order. Lastly, the mean is calculated by adding all the values and dividing by the total number of values, which also results in 5 in this case.

The mode may be used if the data set is qualitative.
Group of answer choices

True

False

True

The mode may be used as a measure of central tendency for qualitative data, which consists of categories or attributes rather than numerical values. In qualitative data, the mode represents the category that occurs most frequently in the data set. It is particularly useful in determining the most common category or attribute in a set of data.

Between the mean and median, which of these is pulled farther in the direction of extreme values or outliers?
Group of answer choices

Median

Mean

Mode

Midrange

Mean

The mean is more sensitive to extreme values or outliers in a data set compared to the median. Outliers can have a significant impact on the mean because it takes into account the actual values of all data points when calculating the average. The median, on the other hand, is less affected by extreme values as it is simply the middle value when the data is ordered.

Sam was in charge of collecting contributions for the Food Bank.  He received donation of $10, $100, $20, $10, $10.  The next contributor wanted to give an amount in line with the other contributions.  If Sam uses the median, what amount will tell the potential contributor?
Group of answer choices

$20

$10

$100

$36

In this case, the contributions are: $10, $100, $20, $10, $10

When the contributions are arranged in ascending order: $10, $10, $10, $20, $100

There are five contributions, so the median will be the middle value. In this case, the middle value is the third value, which is $10.

Therefore, Sam can suggest the potential contributor to give $10 to be in line with the other contributions based on the median. 

So, the correct answer is:

$10

If the frequencies of the data entries in the distribution are equal, what type of histogram is formed when graphed?
Group of answer choices

skewed

symmetric

normally curved

uniformed (rectangular)

uniformed (rectangular)

When the frequencies of the data entries in the distribution are equal, a uniform or rectangular histogram is formed when graphed. This means that each data entry occurs with the same frequency, resulting in equal bars or blocks in the histogram, giving it a uniform appearance.

If there is no middle value in a data set because you have an even number of entries, how do you find the median then?
Group of answer choices

highest minus the lowest values

add the two center observations and divide by two

there is no median in this case

add the highest and the lowest values and divide by two

add the two center observations and divide by two

When there is no single middle value in a data set because of an even number of entries, the median can be found by adding the two center observations and dividing by two. This method calculates the average of the two middle values, providing the median value for the dataset.

If you add the same number to each observation, how does that affect the center of the distributions as measured by the mean or median?
Group of answer choices

The center is multiplied by the number you added.

There is no effect on the center.

The median and the mean will be equal.

The center is increased by the number you added.

The center is increased by the number you added.

Adding the same number to each observation in a data set will increase the center of the distribution as measured by the mean or median by the amount added. This is because the mean and median are measures of central tendency that are influenced by the actual values in the data set, so adding the same amount to each observation will shift the center of the distribution upward by that amount.

If the right and the left sides of a histogram are approximately mirror images of each other, what is the best description of the distribution?
Group of answer choices

Inverse

Right-skewed

Symmetric

Left-skewed

Symmetric

If the right and left sides of a histogram are approximately mirror images of each other, then the best description of the distribution is symmetric. This means that the data is evenly distributed around the center, with equal frequencies on both sides, resulting in a symmetric shape when graphed.
